# N attribute: suppress "don't know how to make" error for recipe-less targets.
# A target with N that doesn't exist and has no recipe should not error.

# Without N, a missing target with no rule is an error.
! mk -n -p 1 -f mkfile.noN
stderr 'don.t know how to make'

# With N and no prereqs: dep doesn't exist, no recipe, no prereqs — OK.
mk -n -p 1 -f mkfile.N all
stdout 'all: echo building'

# With N and prereqs (canonical Plan 9 usage): dep has prereq but no recipe.
mk -n -p 1 -f mkfile.Nprereq all
cmp stdout expected.Nprereq

-- mkfile.noN --
all:V: dep
	echo building

-- mkfile.N --
dep:N:
all:V: dep
	echo building

-- mkfile.Nprereq --
dep:N: source
all:V: dep
	echo building
source:
	echo making source
-- expected.Nprereq --
source: echo making source
all: echo building
